classObjective: Construct dot plots, bar graphs, and histograms to represent data sets.
workedExamples: 9-2, Example 1 — Make a Dot Plot from a Table
9-2, Example 2 — Make a Bar Graph from a Survey
warmUp: What is the difference between a bar graph and a histogram?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: choosing appropriate display type; scaling number lines; discrete vs. continuous data representation
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice constructing dot plots, bar graphs, and histograms
independentPractice: Practice problems constructing data displays and choosing appropriate graph types
assessment: Exit ticket: construct a histogram for a data set with continuous variables and explain why a histogram is appropriate
capReflection: How do you decide which type of graph is most appropriate for a given data set?
